title: hieracium maculatum subsp. arrectarium
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. arrectarium, also known as the spotted hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and acting as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed or applied topically to utilize its medicinal properties.
